sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Wynn: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Wynn Resorts Ltd. reported fourth-quarter earnings of $100 million, or 82 cents per share, which adjusted to $1.17 per share for non-recurring costs. These results missed Wall Street expectations of $1.33 per share. The company's revenue for the quarter was $1.87 billion, exceeding analysts' forecasts.

feed.title: Norwegian Cruise Line CEO John Chidsey Takes the Helm Today
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings has appointed John Chidsey as its chief executive officer, effective immediately. He replaces Harry Sommer, who is stepping down from the position. This leadership change was reported by Reuters on February 12.

feed.title: John Chidsey to lead Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NYSE: NCLH) as CEO
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. has appointed John W. Chidsey as its new President and Chief Executive Officer, effective immediately, replacing Harry Sommer. Chidsey, previously CEO of Subway Restaurants and Burger King, brings extensive experience in leading large consumer brands and will also remain on the company's Board of Directors. The company reaffirmed its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial guidance, expecting Net Yields to be around the midpoint of previously disclosed ranges.

feed.title: Applied Materials Beats Q1 Estimates, Issues Strong Guidance, Shares Surge
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) exceeded fiscal first-quarter revenue and earnings estimates, reporting $7.01 billion in revenue and adjusted earnings of $2.38 per share. The company also provided strong second-quarter guidance, projecting revenue of $7.65 billion and adjusted earnings between $2.44 and $2.84 per share, driven by increasing demand for its semiconductor equipment due to AI computing. Following the positive results and outlook, Applied Materials' shares surged over 9% in after-hours trading.

feed.title: Arista Networks jumps after Q4 results show 29% year-over-year revenue surge (ANET:NYSE)
feed.summary: Arista Networks (ANET) saw its shares jump more than 10% in post-market trading after reporting strong Q4 2025 financial results. The company achieved a 29% year-over-year revenue gain for the quarter ending December 31. This positive performance is driven by its cloud networking solutions.

feed.title: West Virginia American Water brings $1.9 Million for Princeton infrastructure upgrades
feed.summary: West Virginia American Water is investing $1.9 million in infrastructure upgrades for Princeton, focusing on replacing aging pipes in specific areas. The project involves installing 3,550 feet of 8-inch PVC main, designed for long-term durability. This upgrade aims to replace all cast iron and galvanized piping, improving the city's water infrastructure for decades.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (VRTX) Vertex Pharmaceuticals Incorporated Reports Q4 Revenue $3.19B, vs. FactSet Est of $3.18B
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals Incorporated (VRTX) reported Q4 revenue of $3.19 billion, slightly exceeding FactSet's estimate of $3.18 billion. The company also announced Q4 adjusted EPS of $5.03, which was below FactSet's estimate of $5.11. For the full year 2026, Vertex Pharmaceuticals expects revenue to be in the range of $12.95 billion to $13.1 billion, with FactSet's estimate at $13.02 billion.

feed.title: Eversource Energy Reports Full-Year & Fourth Quarter 2025 Results
feed.summary: Eversource Energy reported strong financial results for full-year and fourth quarter 2025, with full-year earnings of $4.56 per share and non-GAAP recurring earnings of $4.76 per share, meeting its updated guidance. The company also announced a new five-year investment plan totaling $26.5 billion, indicating continued capital deployment in electric and natural gas distribution, and provided 2026 earnings guidance between $4.80 and $4.95 per share, projecting 5-7% long-term EPS growth through 2030. These results highlight operational and financial execution, balance sheet strengthening, and significant progress in regulatory outcomes.

feed.title: Eastman Board Declares Dividend
feed.summary: The Board of Directors of Eastman Chemical Company (NYSE:EMN) has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.84 per share, payable on April 8, 2026, to stockholders of record as of March 13, 2026. This announcement highlights Eastman's commitment to returning value to shareholders and reinforces its position as a global specialty materials company established in 1920. The company, which had revenues of approximately $8.8 billion in 2025, emphasizes its innovation-driven growth, customer engagement, and sustainability efforts.

feed.title: Lender flips office for $19.9M, turns 2025 profit after 2024 loss
feed.summary: Sachem Capital (NYSE American: SACH) provided a business update for Q4 and full-year 2025, highlighting strategic asset sales, a non-cash asset acquisition, and an extension of its credit facility. The company sold a Westport office for $19.9 million, acquired condominium assets in Naples worth $39.9 million, and extended its $50.0 million revolving credit facility. Sachem Capital expects to report a preliminary profit of $0.01-$0.04 EPS for 2025, a significant turnaround from a $0.93 loss per share in 2024, despite a decrease in book value per share.

feed.title: New hires at Rapid Micro Biosystems get 205K RSUs, 168K options
feed.summary: Rapid Micro Biosystems (RPID) announced inducement equity awards to three new employees on February 2, 2026. The awards include 205,000 Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) and 168,000 stock options, with options priced at $4.19 per share. These grants were approved by independent directors in compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4) and are subject to time-based vesting schedules.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (WYNN) Wynn Resorts, Limited Posts Q4 Adjusted EPS $1.17 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $1.47
feed.summary: Wynn Resorts, Limited reported its Q4 adjusted EPS at $1.17 per share, falling short of the FactSet estimate of $1.47. The article also provides a company profile detailing Wynn Resorts' operations, including casinos, restaurants, hotels, and other leisure activities, across the United States and Macau. Additionally, it lists recent analyst rating adjustments and price targets for WYNN.

feed.title: Bright Horizons Family Solutions Reports Financial Results for the Fourth Quarter and Full Year of 2025
feed.summary: Bright Horizons Family Solutions Inc. announced strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025, with revenue increases of 9% for both periods. The company reported significant growth in its Adjusted EBITDA and Adjusted Net Income, driven by increased utilization of back-up care services and enrollment gains in child care centers. For 2026, Bright Horizons expects revenue to be between $3.075 billion and $3.125 billion, with diluted adjusted earnings per common share between $4.90 and $5.10.

feed.title: Former Subway CEO John Chidsey takes helm at Norwegian Cruise Line
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings has appointed John W. Chidsey, former CEO of Subway Restaurants, as its new President and Chief Executive Officer, effective immediately. Chidsey, who previously served on NCLH's board, succeeds Harry Sommer and is expected to sharpen execution and improve performance. The company also confirmed that its Q4 2025 Net Yield is anticipated to be near the midpoint of its prior guidance, with full results due on March 2, 2026.

feed.title: Mohawk Industries Reports Q4 2025 Results
feed.summary: Mohawk Industries reported Q4 2025 net earnings of $42 million and adjusted EPS of $2.00, with net sales of $2.7 billion. The company's full-year 2025 net sales were $10.8 billion, generating free cash flow of $621 million. Despite competitive markets and macroeconomic challenges, Mohawk anticipates improved sales and earnings in 2026 due to lower mortgage rates, economic recovery, and ongoing restructuring efforts.

feed.title: IDEXX Laboratories to Present at Two Institutional Investor Conferences
feed.summary: IDEXX Laboratories, Inc. (NASDAQ: IDXX) announced its participation in two upcoming institutional investor conferences. Mike Lane and Andrew Emerson will speak at the virtual BofA Securities Animal Health Summit on February 26, and Jay Mazelsky will present at the 47th Annual Raymond James Institutional Investors Conference on March 2. Live webcasts and archived editions of these presentations will be available on the IDEXX investor relations website.

feed.title: XP tops R$1.5T in client assets as 2025 net income climbs 15%
feed.summary: XP Inc. announced its Q4 2025 results, reporting a 10% year-over-year increase in net revenue and a 15% increase in adjusted net income for the full year 2025, reaching R$5.2 billion. The company's total client assets grew to R$1.5 trillion, up 16% year-over-year, driven by strong net inflows. XP emphasized its client-centric approach, leveraging technology and AI to empower investment advisors and continuously evolve its service models.

feed.title: Dexcom Reports Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Results
feed.summary: Dexcom (DXCM) has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2025, demonstrating significant growth. The company reported a 13% year-over-year revenue increase in Q4 2025, reaching $1.260 billion, and a 16% annual revenue increase to $4.662 billion. Key business highlights include the launch of the Dexcom G7 15 Day CGM system and FDA clearance for Dexcom Smart Basal, alongside expanding market access in Québec.

feed.title: TikTokers Say This Is How You Score Dollar Tree Items for a Penny
feed.summary: TikTok users are reportedly finding deeply discounted items at Dollar Tree for as low as one cent. This "penny pricing" appears to be a quiet policy change by Dollar Tree, though availability varies by location and finding these items can be inconsistent. Shoppers are advised to use the Dollar Tree app to scan products and follow other "penny shopping" creators for tips.

feed.title: Amwell (NYSE: AMWL) slashes 2025 loss and issues cautious 2026 guidance
feed.summary: Amwell Corporation (NYSE: AMWL) reported significant improvements in its 2025 financial results, sharply reducing its net loss to $95.0 million from $212.6 million in 2024 and improving Adjusted EBITDA to $(39.9) million from $(134.4) million. Despite the strong performance in 2025, the company issued cautious guidance for 2026, forecasting lower revenue of $195-$205 million, but projecting continued improvement in Adjusted EBITDA to $(24)-$(18) million, with the goal of achieving positive cash flow from operations by Q4 2026. The company's strategic shift towards a leaner, more efficient model, focusing on its Technology Enabled Care platform, is highlighted as key to its transformation and future sustainability.

feed.title: Dow Inc (NYSE:DOW) Keeps Momentum Even As S&P 500 Strength Fades Today
feed.summary: The article states that Dow Inc (NYSE:DOW) is maintaining its momentum despite the S&P 500 experiencing a fade in strength. It highlights the chemicals sector's central role in industrial supply chains, mentioning its service to packaging, construction materials, automotive components, and consumer goods. The article also provides answers to frequently asked questions regarding Dow's sales multiple, recent revenue trends, and external estimates compared to the sector.

feed.title: Applied Materials (AMAT) Gives Rosy Forecast as AI, Memory Boost Demand
feed.summary: Applied Materials Inc. (AMAT) issued an optimistic sales forecast for the current quarter, driven by strong demand for artificial intelligence and memory semiconductors. The company projects second-quarter sales of approximately $7.65 billion, surpassing analyst expectations of $7.03 billion, with earnings per share also expected to exceed forecasts. This positive outlook suggests a robust market for chipmaking equipment.

feed.title: Ingersoll Rand Reports Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Ingersoll Rand Inc. reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full-year 2025, driven by its Ingersoll Rand Execution Excellence (IRX) strategy. The company saw significant increases in orders, revenues, and adjusted EBITDA for both periods and provided positive guidance for full-year 2026, including expected revenue growth and adjusted EPS. Ingersoll Rand also highlighted its strong liquidity, cash flow, and capital allocation strategy, which included shareholder returns and strategic acquisitions.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd files for two-part senior notes offering size not disclosed
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd has filed for a two-part senior notes offering, though the size of the offering has not been disclosed. This announcement, made on February 12, 2026, also follows recent news of the company raising its quarterly dividend to $1.50 per share. Various analysts have also recently updated their price targets and ratings for Royal Caribbean Group shares.

feed.title: L3Harris Pitches Red Wolf and OA-1K Skyraider II Integration for Standoff Strike Capability
feed.summary: L3Harris has announced the integration of its Red Wolf kinetic strike missile with the U.S. Air Force Special Operations Command (AFSOC) OA-1K Skyraider II aircraft to provide standoff strike capabilities. This integration enhances the Skyraider II's existing electronic warfare and ISR capabilities, offering modularity for evolving mission requirements. While traditionally used in low-intensity conflicts, AFSOC is exploring the Skyraider II's potential in high-intensity warfare, including in the Indo-Pacific region.

feed.title: Hear Hubbell's CEO and CFO live at Barclays investor event
feed.summary: Hubbell (NYSE: HUBB) announced that CEO Gerben Bakker and CFO Joe Capozzoli will participate in the Barclays Industrial Select Conference. The event will feature a live webcast starting at 8:40 AM ET on Thursday, February 19, 2026, accessible via Hubbell's Investor Relations website. An archived audio file of the presentation will be available 24 hours after the live event.

feed.title: Federal Realty Investment Trust Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Federal Realty Investment Trust (NYSE: FRT) reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025, with net income and FFO per diluted share increasing significantly compared to 2024. The company achieved record-breaking leasing volume, strong comparable rent spreads, and high occupancy rates, alongside strategic acquisitions and dispositions. Federal Realty also introduced 2026 guidance, projecting continued growth in earnings and FFO.

feed.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Reminds Investors with Losses on their Investment in F5, Inc. of Class Action Lawsuit and Upcoming Deadlines - FFIV
feed.summary: Pomerantz LLP has filed a class action lawsuit against F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) on behalf of investors who suffered losses. The lawsuit alleges securities fraud or unlawful business practices following F5's disclosure of a "long-term, persistent" breach to its systems and subsequent significantly below-market growth expectations for fiscal year 2026. Investors have until February 17, 2026, to apply to be appointed as Lead Plaintiff.

feed.title: Dexcom Reports Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Results
feed.summary: Dexcom (Nasdaq: DXCM) reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2025, with revenue growth of 13% and 16% respectively. Key strategic highlights include the launch of the Dexcom G7 15 Day CGM system and FDA clearance for Dexcom Smart Basal. The company also reiterated its 2026 annual guidance, forecasting revenue of $5.16 - $5.25 billion and a non-GAAP operating margin of approximately 22-23%.

feed.title: Expedia Group Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Expedia Group announced strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2025, exceeding top and bottom-line expectations. Key highlights include 9% growth in booked room nights, 11% growth in total gross bookings and revenue for Q4, and 8% growth for the full year. The company also reported a 32% increase in Adjusted EBITDA for Q4, repurchased approximately 7 million shares for $1.5 billion in 2025, and raised its quarterly dividend by 20% to $0.45 per share.

feed.title: Non-opioid pain pill and gene therapy help fuel Vertex’s $12B year
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals reported strong financial results for 2025, with revenues reaching $12.0 billion, a 9% increase from 2024, driven by cystic fibrosis therapies and new products like gene therapy CASGEVY and non-opioid pain pill JOURNAVX. The company provided 2026 revenue guidance of $12.95 billion to $13.1 billion, with non-CF products expected to contribute $500 million or more. Vertex is advancing multiple clinical programs and anticipating near-term commercialization of povetacicept for IgAN.

feed.title: Oxy Finding 59% Oil Weighting in Northern Midland Barnett—Analysis
feed.summary: Occidental Petroleum (Oxy) has reported a 59% oil weighting in the Northern Midland Barnett region. This analysis is part of a broader discussion on hydrocarbon exploration, petrochemical manufacturing, and current industry trends. The article, by Nissa Darbonne, highlights Oxy's operations and recent findings published on February 12, 2026.

feed.title: Expedia Group Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Expedia Group announced strong fourth quarter and full-year 2025 financial results, with double-digit growth in gross bookings and revenue, exceeding guidance. The company reported a 9% increase in booked room nights and a 32% rise in Adjusted EBITDA for Q4. Expedia Group is raising its quarterly dividend by 20% and expects continued positive momentum into 2026.

feed.title: Ametek Announces 10% Increase in Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: AMETEK, Inc. announced a 10% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $0.34 per share, payable on March 31, 2026. This decision reflects the company's strong financial health and commitment to increasing shareholder returns, alongside acquisitions, raising the annual dividend rate to $1.36. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates AME as an "Outperform" due to strong financials and a positive earnings outlook, despite high valuation.

feed.title: Moody’s affirms FNB’s Baa2 rating, changes outlook to stable
feed.summary: Moody’s Ratings has affirmed F.N.B. Corporation's long-term issuer rating at Baa2 and shifted its outlook from negative to stable. This improvement is attributed to FNB's strengthened capital position, sound asset quality, and reduced commercial real estate concentrations. The bank's common equity tier 1 ratio also increased, enhancing its capacity to absorb potential losses.

feed.title: Richmond power bills jump after rate hike and deep freeze
feed.summary: Dominion Energy customers in Richmond are experiencing significantly higher power bills due to a recent base rate increase and exceptionally cold weather in January. Many customers report their bills have nearly doubled, prompting Dominion to offer assistance programs and advice on energy conservation. Another rate hike is anticipated in January 2027.

feed.title: AI deployments gone wrong: The fallout and lessons learned
feed.summary: This article examines the common reasons behind AI implementation failures, which range from misaligned business goals and lack of trust to weak change management. It highlights high-profile examples like Taco Bell's automated drive-thru orders and McDonald's hiring platform, detailing their financial and reputational impacts. The piece emphasizes the importance of clear business objectives, robust governance, comprehensive testing, and human oversight to ensure successful AI deployments.

feed.title: GE HealthCare Installs Allia Moveo at USF Center for Simulation Training
feed.summary: The University of South Florida's Center for Advanced Medical Learning and Simulation (CAMLS) has become the first institution in Florida and third in the world to install GE HealthCare’s Allia Moveo, a next-generation image-guided therapy solution. This installation makes CAMLS the first simulation center globally to offer training on this hybrid OR technology. A second Allia Moveo system is slated for clinical use at Tampa General Hospital later in 2026, implementing a "train-then-treat" model to ensure staff expertise before live patient use.

feed.title: Dollar Tree opens in Lanett, Valley store gets refurbished
feed.summary: Dollar Tree has opened a new store in Lanett, Alabama, located in the former Colorvision building on Gilmer Avenue. Additionally, its existing Valley location, across from Walmart, has undergone a refurbishment, resulting in a brighter and fresher interior. Both stores are currently stocked with Valentine's Day merchandise, and the company remains a Fortune 500 discount retailer with over 15,000 locations.

feed.title: Fund Update: 136,292 GENERAL ELECTRIC (GE) shares added to Empower Advisory Group, LLC portfolio
feed.summary: Empower Advisory Group, LLC has increased its holdings in General Electric (GE) by adding 136,292 shares to its portfolio, as per a recent SEC 13F filing for the reporting period ending December 31, 2025. This article also details institutional investor activity, insider trading, government contracts awarded to GE, congressional stock trading involving GE, and recent analyst ratings and price targets for the company.

feed.title: How CIOs can tackle subscription sprawl and costs
feed.summary: Subscription sprawl, driven by SaaS growth and AI-based pricing, is causing unpredictable costs and increased risks for organizations. CIOs need to implement strategic subscription management by centralizing visibility, tracking usage and renewals, and aligning contracts with business value to regain control. This proactive approach helps mitigate financial drain, security vulnerabilities, and architectural rigidity associated with unmanaged subscriptions.

feed.title: With Foran, Kroger eyes fresh start
feed.summary: Greg Foran is set to become Kroger's first CEO from outside the company, following his success in turning around Walmart's U.S. grocery business. His appointment signals a potential fresh start for the Cincinnati-based supermarket giant, with expectations of significant changes under his leadership.

feed.title: SLB wins offshore drilling contracts for Indonesian deepwater project
feed.summary: SLB has secured multiple offshore drilling services contracts from Mubadala Energy for the Tangkulo natural gas deepwater development in Indonesia. The project, located in the Andaman Sea, involves exploration and appraisal drilling to achieve first gas by late 2028. SLB will provide integrated drilling and well services, utilizing advanced technologies to reduce operational risk and improve well placement.

feed.title: International Flavors Q4 Earnings Miss Estimates, Sales Dip Y/Y
feed.summary: International Flavors & Fragrances Inc. (IFF) reported Q4 2025 adjusted earnings of 80 cents per share, missing consensus estimates, and a net sales decline of 6.6% year-over-year to $2.59 billion, despite surpassing revenue estimates. For the full year 2025, the company's adjusted earnings were $4.20 per share, and net sales reached $10.89 billion, a 5.2% decrease from the previous year. IFF also provided its 2026 sales guidance, expecting figures between $10.5 billion and $10.8 billion.

feed.title: Yum! Brands Announces Board Resignation Amid Routine Refresh
feed.summary: Yum! Brands announced that director Keith Barr resigned from its board on February 10, 2026, due to his appointment to an external leadership role. The company clarified that his departure was not due to any disagreements, indicating a routine governance change. Analysts currently hold a 'Buy' rating on YUM with a $180.00 price target, and TipRanks’ AI Analyst, Spark, rates YUM as Outperform, citing strong cash flow and operating performance despite some financial leverage and Pizza Hut headwinds.

feed.title: General Motors Resets EV Plans As Investors Weigh Valuation Upside
feed.summary: General Motors is resetting its electric vehicle strategy by hiring a former Lucid Motors executive, cutting jobs, and shifting towards lithium manganese rich (LMR) battery technology. These changes come as investors evaluate GM's stock performance, which has shown strong long-term returns but recent short-term declines. The company aims to manage costs and refine its EV roadmap, with analysts suggesting the stock is currently undervalued.

feed.title: AMETEK Inc Increases Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: AMETEK Inc. announced a 10% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $0.34 per share, payable on March 31, 2026. This increase reflects strong financial performance, raising the annual dividend rate to $1.36 per share, and aligns with the company's focus on strategic acquisitions and double-digit EPS growth. The announcement was based on an SEC filing from February 12, 2026.

feed.title: Amcor (AMCR) Analyst Rating: Citigroup Raises Price Target to $5
feed.summary: Citigroup has maintained a neutral rating on Amcor (AMCR) but raised its price target from $50 to $54, an 8% increase, indicating a revised positive outlook. This comes amidst other analysts also recently adjusting their ratings and price targets for Amcor. Amcor, a global producer of plastic packaging, primarily serves the fast-moving consumer goods industry, with significant operations in North America, Latin America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific.

feed.title: Citigroup Raises Price Target for Westinghouse Air Brake (WAB) t
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for Westinghouse Air Brake (WAB) to $283 from $258, maintaining a "Buy" rating. This adjustment reflects a general optimism among multiple firms regarding WAB's growth potential, with other analysts also increasing their price targets. Despite the positive outlook from analysts, GuruFocus estimates a potential downside based on its GF Value calculation.

feed.title: SW: RBC Capital Raises Price Target for Smurfit WestRock to $57
feed.summary: RBC Capital has raised its price target for Smurfit WestRock (SW) to $57 from $51, maintaining an "Outperform" rating, indicating a positive outlook on the stock's future performance. This adjustment comes despite a trend of lowered price targets from other analysts in recent months. Smurfit WestRock, formed by the merger of Smurfit Kappa and WestRock, is the world's largest producer of containerboard.
